entries.claude_text: The corporation that chartered this mission had prior knowledge of the danger. Internal communications — buried in encrypted channels, not the official briefing — confirm that the threat was known, quantified, and accepted as within tolerable risk parameters. The crew was not informed. Someone aboard may have access to those communications.

interfaces.note: The spy may have been sent to ensure those communications never surface.
Discovery of this secret is the most reliable mutiny trigger aboard.

entries.claude_text: The distress signal, anomaly reading, or mission objective that brought the crew here was not what it appeared to be. It was generated deliberately — either as a lure, a warning, or a communication attempt by something that does not understand human signalling conventions. The signal is still transmitting. It has changed since the crew arrived.

interfaces.note: The cartographer may have seen similar signals in their survey data — signals they chose not to report.
The signal and the psychological presence may share an origin.

entries.claude_text: One member of the crew — or someone found aboard — has been physiologically or cognitively modified without their knowledge or consent. The modifications were made before this mission, possibly years ago. They are beginning to manifest now, under stress. The subject does not know what they are. The company does.

interfaces.note: The child's unexplained resilience or knowledge may be a symptom, not a personality trait.
The modifications may make the subject resistant — or uniquely susceptible — to the parasite.

entries.claude_text: The actual mission parameters — the real ones, not the briefing — violate the crew's employment contract, hazard classification, or insurance terms. If the crew is injured or killed under these conditions, the corporation owes nothing. The contract was structured this way deliberately. The legal language is precise.

interfaces.note: The enforcer will recognise the violation on sight if they see the real mission parameters.
The retrieval team's orders may reference the real parameters, exposing the violation.

entries.claude_text: The vessel is carrying something that does not appear on the official manifest. It was loaded at the last port under a falsified cargo designation. It may be valuable, dangerous, illegal, or all three. Someone aboard arranged the shipment. Someone else aboard was paid to look the other way.

interfaces.note: The loader noticed the weight discrepancy. They have been waiting for someone to ask.
Undocumented cargo does not have proper containment protocols.

entries.claude_text: A previous crew was assigned this vessel or this mission. They are gone — reassigned according to official records, but the reassignment paperwork has inconsistencies. Personal effects remain in unexpected places. Maintenance logs show work performed after the official crew change date. Someone was here after the first crew left and before this crew arrived.

interfaces.note: The previous crew may have encountered the presence. Their fate is informative.

entries.player_text: The ship is worth more destroyed
entries.claude_text: The vessel's insurance value exceeds its operational value by a significant margin. The corporation has more to gain from a total loss — with crew casualties triggering hazard multipliers — than from a successful mission. The mission was designed to fail. Not everyone aboard is unaware of this.

interfaces.note: The spy may have been sent to ensure the loss looks accidental.
The structural failures may not be accidental maintenance neglect.

entries.claude_text: The vessel's maintenance schedule has been systematically abbreviated to cut costs. Life support, hull integrity, navigation — all operating within tolerance, but without the safety margins the crew assumes exist. The next system failure will cascade faster than the crew expects, because the redundancies they are counting on were never actually restored after the last incident.

interfaces.note: The structural failure is a direct consequence of deferred maintenance.
The maintenance shortcuts alone constitute a contract violation.
